rough_idea: The store manager needs to easily add new construction materials into the inventory with all necessary details such as name, quantity, and supplier.The storekeeper wants to update stock levels whenever materials are received from suppliers or issued to construction sites.The project manager needs to track how much material is being used for each construction project.The site engineer wants to request materials from the inventory and record the quantities received at the site.The inventory manager needs to be notified when stock levels of important materials become lowThe purchasing officer wants to maintain accurate records of suppliers and the materials they provide.The management team needs to generate reports showing current stock levels and material usage across projects.The storekeeper wants to record returned materials when unused items are sent back from construction sites.The project manager needs to transfer materials between construction sites when required.The administrator wants to control access
user_stories:
As an experienced stakeholder representative, I have analyzed the system requirements and user roles. To ensure the system is robust, user-friendly, and meets the complex operational needs of a construction environment, I have developed a comprehensive set of detailed user stories.

These stories cover the full user journey, from initial inventory intake to final project reporting, and include necessary non-functional requirements (NFRs) for system stability and security.

---

## 🏗️ I. Inventory Management & Stock Control (Store Manager, Storekeeper, Inventory Manager)

These stories focus on the core physical movement and tracking of materials.

| User Story | Type | Context & Justification |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **As a Store Manager,** I want to add a new construction material item with mandatory details (Name, SKU/Barcode, Unit of Measure, Initial Quantity, Safety Stock Level, and Supplier ID) **so that** the inventory system is accurate and I can track all incoming goods immediately. | Functional | **Detail:** The system must enforce unique SKUs/Barcodes. The 'Safety Stock Level' is critical for the Inventory Manager's alerts. **Edge Case:** Must handle materials that are measured by volume (e.g., cubic meters) vs. count (e.g., units). |
| **As a Storekeeper,** I want to record a material receipt against a specific Purchase Order (PO) and Supplier **so that** the inventory stock levels are automatically updated and I have a verifiable audit trail of the incoming goods. | Functional | **Detail:** The system must allow batch entry of received items and link the receipt to the PO number. **Justification:** This prevents manual stock inflation and ensures accountability. |
| **As a Storekeeper,** I want to record the issuance of materials to a specific Project ID and Site Location, specifying the quantity and the reason code (e.g., 'Usage', 'Transfer', 'Testing'), **so that** the inventory is accurately deducted and I know exactly where the material went. | Functional | **Detail:** The system must require a mandatory Project ID and a signature/digital confirmation upon issuance. **Edge Case:** Must handle partial issue (e.g., 100 units requested, 95 units issued). |
| **As a Storekeeper,** I want to record returned materials, specifying the Project ID and the reason for return (e.g., 'Damaged', 'Unused', 'Wrong Item'), **so that** the inventory count is corrected, and the Project Manager is aware of the discrepancy. | Functional | **Detail:** The system must allow the storekeeper to inspect and categorize the returned item (e.g., 'Returnable Stock' vs. 'Scrap'). |
| **As an Inventory Manager,** I want to set and view customizable reorder points (Safety Stock Levels) for each material, and receive an automated notification (email/dashboard alert) when the stock falls below this threshold **so that** I can proactively initiate purchasing before running out of critical materials. | Functional / NFR | **Detail:** The notification must include the material name, current stock level, and the required reorder quantity. **Justification:** This is the core preventative maintenance function of the system. |

## 👷 II. Project & Site Operations (Project Manager, Site Engineer)

These stories focus on the consumption and movement of materials at the operational level.

| User Story | Type | Context & Justification |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **As a Site Engineer,** I want to submit a formal Material Request Form specifying the required materials, quantities, and the exact construction task/area, linked to a specific Project ID, **so that** the Storekeeper can process the request efficiently and I have a documented record of my needs. | Functional | **Detail:** The request must be viewable by the Storekeeper and Project Manager. The system should allow for priority flagging (e.g., 'Urgent'). |
| **As a Site Engineer,** I want to view the status of my material requests (e.g., 'Pending Approval', 'Approved', 'Issued', 'Rejected') **so that** I can manage site workflow and adjust my schedule accordingly. | Functional | **Detail:** This provides transparency and reduces time wasted waiting for materials. |
| **As a Project Manager,** I want to view a dashboard summarizing the material usage rate (consumption vs. budgeted usage) for all active projects, broken down by material type, **so that** I can identify projects that are over-consuming or under-utilizing resources. | Functional | **Detail:** This requires linking material usage to project milestones or budgeted consumption rates. |
| **As a Project Manager,** I want to initiate a material transfer request between two specific Project IDs/Site Locations, specifying the materials and quantities, **so that** I can ensure materials are moved efficiently without manual paperwork. | Functional | **Detail:** The system must require approval from both the originating and receiving site/manager. |

## 💰 III. Procurement & Finance (Purchasing Officer, Management Team)

These stories focus on the financial, supplier, and reporting aspects.

| User Story | Type | Context & Justification |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **As a Purchasing Officer,** I want to maintain a centralized, searchable database of suppliers, including contact details, service agreements, material specialties, and performance ratings (e.g., 'On-Time Delivery Rate'), **so that** I can select the most reliable and cost-effective vendor for any material. | Functional | **Detail:** The system should allow for historical tracking of supplier performance (e.g., number of complaints, average delivery delay). |
| **As a Purchasing Officer,** I want to generate a Purchase Requisition (PR) based on low stock alerts or project needs, which can then be converted into a formal Purchase Order (PO) and sent to the selected supplier, **so that** the procurement process is standardized and auditable. | Functional | **Detail:** The PO must reference the specific materials, quantities, and the agreed-upon pricing structure. |
| **As a Management Team Member,** I want to generate comprehensive reports detailing: 1) Current stock valuation, 2) Material usage per project over a defined period, and 3) Total cost of materials consumed, **so that** I can assess profitability, optimize inventory levels, and make strategic business decisions. | Functional | **Detail:** Reports must be exportable (CSV/PDF) and filterable by date range, project, and material category. |
| **As a Management Team Member,** I want to view a historical audit log of all inventory transactions (receipts, issues, returns, transfers), including the user who performed the action and the timestamp, **so that** I can investigate discrepancies and ensure accountability. | Functional / NFR | **Detail:** This is crucial for financial auditing and dispute resolution. |

## 🛡️ IV. System Administration & Non-Functional Requirements (Administrator)

These stories ensure the system is secure, reliable, and scalable.

| User Story | Type | Context & Justification |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **As an Administrator,** I want to define and manage user roles and permissions (Role-Based Access Control - RBAC), ensuring that users can only access the specific functions and data relevant to their job role (e.g., Site Engineers cannot modify supplier details), **so that** system integrity and data security are maintained. | Functional / NFR | **Detail:** Must include granular control (e.g., Storekeeper can *issue* but cannot *add* new materials). |
| **As an Administrator,** I want the system to automatically back up all transaction data daily and maintain data integrity checks, **so that** the business can recover quickly from system failures or data corruption. | Non-Functional | **Detail:** Requires robust backup protocols and version control for critical data (e.g., material pricing). |
| **As a System User,** I want the system interface to be intuitive, requiring minimal training, and to provide clear, contextual help prompts, **so that** I can perform my tasks efficiently and reduce operational errors. | Non-
qa_pairs:
Q1: ### Question 1: **What are the specific criteria for supplier performance ratings, and how will these ratings impact material selection?** **Rationale:** This question is crucial because it directly impacts the functional requirement of maintaining a centralized database of suppliers. Understanding the criteria for supplier performance ratings (e.g., on-time delivery rate, quality standards) ensures that the system can accurately assess and prioritize suppliers based on their reliability and cost-effectiveness. Additionally, knowing how these ratings influence material selection will help in designing the user interface and workflows to ensure that users are guided towards making informed decisions.  ### Question 2: **How should partial issuance of materials be handled in terms of inventory adjustments and documentation?** **Rationale:** This question is important because it addresses an edge case (partial issue) that needs clear handling. It ensures that the system can accurately reflect changes in inventory levels, maintain audit trails, and prevent discrepancies. Understanding the process will help in designing robust validation rules and user interfaces to ensure that partial issuances are correctly recorded and documented.  ### Question 3: **What specific data fields should be included in the historical audit log for inventory transactions?** **Rationale:** This question is critical because it ensures comprehensive traceability of all inventory actions. The detailed information required (e.g., user ID, timestamp, action type) will help in maintaining accountability and facilitating investigations into discrepancies. It also impacts non-functional requirements related to system integrity and security.  ### Question 4: **What are the specific roles and permissions for each user type, and how do they interact with different components of the system?** **Rationale:** This question is essential because it clarifies the role-based access control (RBAC) requirements. Defining the exact roles and their interactions ensures that the system can enforce granular controls effectively, maintaining data security and integrity. It also impacts the design of user interfaces and workflows to ensure that users have only the necessary permissions.  ### Question 5: **How should the system handle material requests that exceed safety stock levels?** **Rationale:** This question addresses a potential edge case where users might request materials beyond their current safety stock levels. Understanding how the system will manage such requests (e.g., automatically generate purchase orders, notify management) ensures that the system can handle unexpected surges in demand without manual intervention.  ### Prioritization: 1. **Question 1:** Understanding supplier performance ratings is critical for effective procurement management. 2. **Question 2:** Handling partial issuances ensures accurate inventory tracking and audit trails. 3. **Question 3:** Comprehensive audit logs are essential for maintaining system integrity and security. 4. **Question 5:** Defining how to handle material requests that exceed safety stock levels ensures robust inventory management.  These questions will significantly impact the design and implementation of the system by ensuring that all functional and non-functional requirements are clearly defined, leading to a more robust and user-friendly solution.
A1: Supplier performance ratings will be based on delivery time, material quality, price, and reliability. Suppliers with higher ratings will be preferred when selecting materials.If only part of the requested material is issued, the system will reduce the issued quantity from stock and record the remaining quantity as pending.The audit log will store user name, date and time, action type, material name, quantity, and project or location details.Different users will have different roles such as admin, storekeeper, and project manager, and each role will have access only to allowed functions.If a request exceeds the available stock, the system will show an alert and notify the responsible person to reorder materials.
req_draft:
### I. Inventory Management & Stock Control

#### Functional Requirements (FR)
1. **FR-001: Add New Material Item**
   - **Title:** Add a new construction material item with mandatory details.
   - **Description:** The system must allow the addition of a new material item, requiring fields such as Name, SKU/Barcode, Unit of Measure, Initial Quantity, Safety Stock Level, and Supplier ID. Unique SKUs/Barcodes are enforced.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - The system enforces unique SKUs/Barcodes for each material item.
     - The 'Safety Stock Level' triggers automated alerts when stock falls below this threshold.
     - Materials measured by volume (e.g., cubic meters) and count (e.g., units) are handled appropriately.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 1

2. **FR-002: Record Material Receipt**
   - **Title:** Record a material receipt against a specific Purchase Order (PO) and Supplier.
   - **Description:** The system must allow the recording of received materials, linking them to a PO number for verifiable audit trails.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Batch entry of received items is supported.
     - Receipts are automatically linked to the corresponding PO number.
     - System updates inventory stock levels upon receipt.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 2

3. **FR-003: Record Material Issuance**
   - **Title:** Record issuance of materials to a specific Project ID and Site Location.
   - **Description:** The system must allow the recording of material issuances, specifying the quantity and reason code (e.g., 'Usage', 'Transfer', 'Testing').
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Mandatory fields include Project ID and signature/digital confirmation upon issuance.
     - Partial issue scenarios are handled correctly.
     - System updates inventory stock levels accordingly.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 3

4. **FR-004: Record Material Returns**
   - **Title:** Record returned materials with Project ID and reason for return.
   - **Description:** The system must allow the recording of material returns, categorizing them as 'Returnable Stock' or 'Scrap'.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Returned items are inspected and categorized correctly.
     - System updates inventory stock levels based on return status.
     - Returns are linked to specific Project IDs for accountability.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 4

5. **FR-005: Set Reorder Points**
   - **Title:** Set and view customizable reorder points (Safety Stock Levels) for each material.
   - **Description:** The system must allow setting and viewing of customizable reorder points, with automated notifications when stock falls below the threshold.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Notifications include material name, current stock level, and required reorder quantity.
     - Automated alerts are sent via email or dashboard.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 5

#### Non-Functional Requirements (NFR)
1. **NFR-001: System Stability**
   - **Title:** Ensure system stability under high load.
   - **Description:** The system must maintain performance and reliability even when handling large volumes of transactions.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - System can handle up to 5,000 transactions per minute without degradation in performance.
     - Daily backups are performed automatically.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 1

2. **NFR-002: Security**
   - **Title:** Ensure data security and integrity.
   - **Description:** The system must implement robust security measures to protect sensitive information.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) is enforced for all users.
     - Data encryption is used for storing sensitive data.
     - Audit logs are maintained for all transactions.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 4

3. **NFR-003: Usability**
   - **Title:** Ensure the system interface is intuitive and user-friendly.
   - **Description:** The system must have an easy-to-use interface that requires minimal training for users.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Clear, contextual help prompts are provided throughout the application.
     - User roles and permissions are clearly defined in the UI.
     - System provides real-time feedback on user actions.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 1

4. **NFR-004: Scalability**
   - **Title:** Ensure system scalability to accommodate future growth.
   - **Description:** The system must be designed to handle increased data and transaction volumes as the business grows.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - System can scale horizontally by adding more servers.
     - Data storage is optimized for performance and efficiency.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 1

### II. Project & Site Operations

#### Functional Requirements (FR)
6. **FR-006: Submit Material Request Form**
   - **Title:** Submit a formal Material Request Form specifying required materials, quantities, and construction task/area.
   - **Description:** The system must allow users to submit material requests linked to specific Project IDs.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Requests are viewable by Storekeepers and Project Managers.
     - Priority flags (e.g., 'Urgent') can be set for requests.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 6

7. **FR-007: View Material Request Status**
   - **Title:** View the status of submitted material requests.
   - **Description:** The system must provide a view of the current status and history of all material requests.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Users can track the progress of their requests in real-time.
     - Historical records are maintained for auditing purposes.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 6

#### Non-Functional Requirements (NFR)
1. **NFR-005: Performance**
   - **Title:** Ensure fast response times for critical operations.
   - **Description:** The system must provide quick responses to critical operations such as material requests and receipts.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Average response time for critical operations is less than 2 seconds.
     - System can handle up to 1,000 concurrent users without performance degradation.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** User Story 6

### III. Inventory Management & Stock Control (continued)

#### Functional Requirements (FR)
8. **FR-008: Handle Partial Issuances**
   - **Title:** Handle partial issuances of materials accurately.
   - **Description:** The system must correctly handle scenarios where only part of the requested material is issued.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - System reduces issued quantity from stock and records remaining quantity as pending.
     - Notifications are sent to responsible parties when partial issuance occurs.
   - **Priority:** Medium
   - **Source:** Q&A 2

#### Non-Functional Requirements (NFR)
1. **NFR-006: Data Integrity**
   - **Title:** Ensure data integrity and consistency.
   - **Description:** The system must maintain accurate and consistent data across all transactions.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Automated validation rules ensure data accuracy.
     - Regular data checks are performed to identify inconsistencies.
   - **Priority:** Medium
   - **Source:** Q&A 2

### IV. Historical Audit Logs

#### Functional Requirements (FR)
9. **FR-009: Maintain Comprehensive Audit Logs**
   - **Title:** Maintain comprehensive audit logs for all inventory transactions.
   - **Description:** The system must maintain detailed records of all inventory actions, including user ID, date and time, action type, material name, quantity, and project or location details.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Audit logs are generated automatically for all inventory actions.
     - Logs can be easily queried and exported for reporting purposes.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** Q&A 3

#### Non-Functional Requirements (NFR)
1. **NFR-007: Security**
   - **Title:** Ensure audit logs are secure and accessible only to authorized personnel.
   - **Description:** The system must restrict access to audit logs based on user roles.
   - **Acceptance Criteria:**
     - Only users with appropriate permissions can view audit logs.
     - Audit logs are encrypted for additional security.
   - **Priority:** High
   - **Source:** Q&A 3

### V. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)

#### Functional Requirements (FR)
10. **FR-010: Define User Roles and Permissions**
    - **Title:** Define user roles and permissions within the system.
    - **Description:** The system must allow defining different user roles with specific permissions for accessing various components of the application.
    - **Acceptance Criteria:**
      - Admin, Storekeeper, Project Manager, and other roles are defined.
      - Each role has access only to allowed functions.
      - Role definitions can be updated dynamically as needed.
    - **Priority:** High
    - **Source:** Q&A 4

#### Non-Functional Requirements (NFR)
1. **NFR-008: Scalability**
   - **Title:** Ensure RBAC is scalable and flexible.
   - **Description:** The system must support adding or modifying roles and permissions without significant downtime.
   - **Accept
entities:
### Actors (Users, External Systems, Stakeholders)

1. **Actor: Storekeeper**
   - Name: Storekeeper
   - Type: Actor
   - Description: A user responsible for managing inventory levels, issuing materials, receiving returns, and maintaining stock records.
   - Attributes: None specified in the requirements; typically includes roles such as "Storekeeper" with specific permissions.
   - Role: Manages inventory, issues materials to projects, receives material returns, sets reorder points.

2. **Actor: Project Manager**
   - Name: Project Manager
   - Type: Actor
   - Description: A user responsible for overseeing project-related activities and ensuring that the correct materials are requested and issued.
   - Attributes: None specified in the requirements; typically includes roles such as "Project Manager" with specific permissions.
   - Role: Submits material requests, tracks request status, approves or rejects requests.

3. **Actor: Supplier**
   - Name: Supplier
   - Type: External System
   - Description: An external entity that provides construction materials and is linked to the system through Purchase Orders (POs).
   - Attributes: None specified in the requirements; typically includes fields such as "Supplier ID," "Contact Information."
   - Role: Provides materials, issues POs, receives receipts.

4. **Actor: User**
   - Name: User
   - Type: Actor
   - Description: A generic term for any user interacting with the system, including Storekeepers and Project Managers.
   - Attributes: None specified in the requirements; typically includes fields such as "User ID," "Role."
   - Role: Interacts with various system functionalities based on their role.

### Data Objects (Entities, Files, Databases, Information)

1. **Data Object: Material Item**
   - Name: Material Item
   - Type: Data Object
   - Description: Represents a specific construction material item in the inventory.
   - Attributes: 
     - Name
     - SKU/Barcode
     - Unit of Measure (e.g., cubic meters, units)
     - Initial Quantity
     - Safety Stock Level
     - Supplier ID
   - Constraints:
     - Unique SKUs/Barcodes are enforced.

2. **Data Object: Purchase Order (PO)**
   - Name: Purchase Order (PO)
   - Type: Data Object
   - Description: Represents a purchase order issued by the system to suppliers.
   - Attributes: 
     - PO Number
     - Supplier ID
     - Date Issued
     - Status (e.g., Open, Closed)
   - Constraints:
     - Linked to specific materials and projects.

3. **Data Object: Material Receipt**
   - Name: Material Receipt
   - Type: Data Object
   - Description: Represents the receipt of materials into inventory.
   - Attributes: 
     - PO Number
     - Date Received
     - Quantity Received
     - Supplier ID
   - Constraints:
     - Linked to specific materials and projects.

4. **Data Object: Material Issuance**
   - Name: Material Issuance
   - Type: Data Object
   - Description: Represents the issuance of materials from inventory.
   - Attributes: 
     - Project ID
     - Date Issued
     - Quantity Issued
     - Reason Code (e.g., Usage, Transfer, Testing)
   - Constraints:
     - Linked to specific materials and projects.

5. **Data Object: Material Return**
   - Name: Material Return
   - Type: Data Object
   - Description: Represents the return of materials from a project.
   - Attributes: 
     - Project ID
     - Date Returned
     - Quantity Returned
     - Reason for Return (e.g., Returnable Stock, Scrap)
   - Constraints:
     - Linked to specific materials and projects.

6. **Data Object: Reorder Point**
   - Name: Reorder Point
   - Type: Data Object
   - Description: Represents the customizable reorder point set by users.
   - Attributes: 
     - Material ID
     - Safety Stock Level
     - Date Set
   - Constraints:
     - Linked to specific materials.

7. **Data Object: Audit Log**
   - Name: Audit Log
   - Type: Data Object
   - Description: Represents a record of all inventory transactions.
   - Attributes: 
     - User ID
     - Date and Time
     - Action Type (e.g., Add, Issue, Return)
     - Material Name
     - Quantity
     - Project or Location Details
   - Constraints:
     - Linked to specific materials and projects.

### Processes (Functions, Services, Workflows, Operations)

1. **Process: Add New Material Item**
   - Name: Add New Material Item
   - Type: Process
   - Description: Adds a new material item to the inventory.
   - Inputs: 
     - Name
     - SKU/Barcode
     - Unit of Measure
     - Initial Quantity
     - Safety Stock Level
     - Supplier ID
   - Outputs: 
     - Confirmation message
     - Updated Inventory Record
   - Triggers: User action (e.g., form submission)

2. **Process: Record Material Receipt**
   - Name: Record Material Receipt
   - Type: Process
   - Description: Records the receipt of materials into inventory.
   - Inputs: 
     - PO Number
     - Quantity Received
     - Supplier ID
   - Outputs: 
     - Updated Inventory Record
     - Confirmation message
   - Triggers: User action (e.g., form submission)

3. **Process: Record Material Issuance**
   - Name: Record Material Issuance
   - Type: Process
   - Description: Records the issuance of materials from inventory.
   - Inputs: 
     - Project ID
     - Quantity Issued
     - Reason Code
   - Outputs: 
     - Updated Inventory Record
     - Confirmation message
   - Triggers: User action (e.g., form submission)

4. **Process: Record Material Return**
   - Name: Record Material Return
   - Type: Process
   - Description: Records the return of materials to inventory.
   - Inputs: 
     - Project ID
     - Quantity Returned
     - Reason for Return
   - Outputs: 
     - Updated Inventory Record
     - Confirmation message
   - Triggers: User action (e.g., form submission)

5. **Process: Set Reorder Points**
   - Name: Set Reorder Points
   - Type: Process
   - Description: Sets and views customizable reorder points for materials.
   - Inputs: 
     - Material ID
     - Safety Stock Level
   - Outputs: 
     - Updated Reorder Point Record
     - Confirmation message
   - Triggers: User action (e.g., form submission)

6. **Process: Submit Material Request Form**
   - Name: Submit Material Request Form
   - Type: Process
   - Description: Submits a material request for a project.
   - Inputs: 
     - Project ID
     - Materials Required
     - Quantity
   - Outputs: 
     - Confirmation message
     - Updated Request Record
   - Triggers: User action (e.g., form submission)

7. **Process: Track Material Request Status**
   - Name: Track Material Request Status
   - Type: Process
   - Description: Tracks the status of a material request.
   - Inputs: 
     - Request ID
   - Outputs: 
     - Request Status
   - Triggers: User action (e.g., form submission)

8. **Process: Define User Roles and Permissions**
   - Name: Define User Roles and Permissions
   - Type: Process
   - Description: Defines user roles and permissions within the system.
   - Inputs: 
     - Role Definition
     - Permission Set
   - Outputs: 
     - Updated Role Definitions
     - Confirmation message
   - Triggers: Admin action (e.g., form submission)

### System Components (Modules, Services, Interfaces)

1. **System Component: Inventory Management Module**
   - Name: Inventory Management Module
   - Type: System Component
   - Description: Manages the inventory of construction materials.
   - Responsibilities: 
     - Add new material items
     - Record receipts and issuances
     - Set reorder points
     - Maintain audit logs
   - Interfaces: 
     - Communicates with other modules for data exchange

2. **System Component: Request Management Module**
   - Name: Request Management Module
   - Type: System Component
   - Description: Manages material requests from projects.
   - Responsibilities: 
     - Submit and track material requests
     - Approve or reject requests
   - Interfaces: 
     - Communicates with the inventory management module for data exchange

3. **System Component: RBAC Module**
   - Name: RBAC Module
   - Type: System Component
   - Description: Manages user roles and permissions.
   - Responsibilities: 
     - Define and update role definitions
     - Assign permissions to users
   - Interfaces: 
     - Communicates with other modules for data exchange

4. **System Component: Audit Log Module**
   - Name: Audit Log Module
   - Type: System Component
   - Description: Manages the creation and storage of audit logs.
   - Responsibilities: 
     - Record all inventory transactions
     - Securely store audit logs
   - Interfaces: 
     - Communicates with other modules for data exchange

5. **System Component: Supplier Interface**
   - Name: Supplier Interface
   - Type: System Component
   - Description: Manages interactions with suppliers.
   - Responsibilities: 
     - Issue and receive purchase orders
     - Manage supplier information
   - Interfaces: 
     - Communicates with the inventory management module for data exchange

These entities provide a
relationships:
### 1. ASSOCIATION RELATIONSHIPS (uses, interacts with, accesses)

#### Association: "uses"
- **Source Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Target Entity:** Project
- **Relationship Type:** Association
- **Relationship Name:** uses
- **Cardinality:** 1:many
- **Description:** A material request form is used by a project to specify required materials and quantities.

#### Association: "manages"
- **Source Entity:** Storekeeper
- **Target Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Relationship Type:** Association
- **Relationship Name:** manages
- **Cardinality:** 1:many
- **Description:** A storekeeper manages multiple material request forms submitted by various projects.

#### Association: "views"
- **Source Entity:** ProjectManager
- **Target Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Relationship Type:** Association
- **Relationship Name:** views
- **Cardinality:** 1:many
- **Description:** A project manager views the status and history of multiple material request forms.

#### Association: "accesses"
- **Source Entity:** User
- **Target Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Relationship Type:** Association
- **Relationship Name:** accesses
- **Cardinality:** 1:many
- **Description:** A user can access multiple material request forms based on their role and permissions.

### 2. COMPOSITION RELATIONSHIPS (contains, consists of, includes)

#### Composition: "consists of"
- **Source Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Target Entity:** RequestedMaterial
- **Relationship Type:** Composition
- **Relationship Name:** consists of
- **Cardinality:** many:many
- **Description:** A material request form consists of multiple requested materials, and each material can be part of multiple forms.

#### Composition: "contains"
- **Source Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Target Entity:** RequestedQuantity
- **Relationship Type:** Composition
- **Relationship Name:** contains
- **Cardinality:** 1:many
- **Description:** A material request form contains multiple requested quantities, each associated with a specific material.

### 3. DEPENDENCY RELATIONSHIPS (depends on, requires, needs)

#### Dependency: "requires"
- **Source Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Target Entity:** Project
- **Relationship Type:** Dependency
- **Relationship Name:** requires
- **Strength:** strong
- **Description:** A material request form depends strongly on the project it is associated with for context and purpose.

#### Dependency: "depends on"
- **Source Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Target Entity:** Storekeeper
- **Relationship Type:** Dependency
- **Relationship Name:** depends on
- **Strength:** strong
- **Description:** A material request form depends strongly on the storekeeper who manages it for approval and issuance.

#### Dependency: "requires"
- **Source Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Target Entity:** RequestedMaterial
- **Relationship Type:** Dependency
- **Relationship Name:** requires
- **Strength:** strong
- **Description:** A material request form requires specific requested materials to be valid and actionable.

### 4. INHERITANCE RELATIONSHIPS (is a type of, specializes)

#### Inheritance: "is a type of"
- **Source Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Target Entity:** RequestForm
- **Relationship Type:** Inheritance
- **Relationship Name:** is a type of
- **Description:** A material request form is a specific type of request form, inheriting common attributes and behaviors.

### 5. FLOW RELATIONSHIPS (data flow, control flow, communication)

#### Flow: "data flow"
- **Source Entity:** ProjectManager
- **Target Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Relationship Type:** Flow
- **Flow Type:** data
- **Flow Content:** Request status updates and approvals
- **Direction:** bidirectional
- **Description:** Data flows between the project manager and material request forms for approval and status updates.

#### Flow: "control flow"
- **Source Entity:** Storekeeper
- **Target Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Relationship Type:** Flow
- **Flow Type:** control
- **Flow Content:** Issuance of materials based on approved requests
- **Direction:** unidirectional
- **Description:** Control flows from the storekeeper to material request forms for issuing requested materials.

#### Flow: "message flow"
- **Source Entity:** User
- **Target Entity:** MaterialRequestForm
- **Relationship Type:** Flow
- **Flow Type:** message
- **Flow Content:** Notifications and updates about requests
- **Direction:** bidirectional
- **Description:** Messages flow between users and material request forms for notifications and status updates.

These relationships ensure that the system is logically consistent, scalable, and meets the requirements of the construction domain.
check_results:
Based on the provided user stories and analysis, I will address each of the requested areas.

**1. COMPLETENESS ANALYSIS**

Missing functional requirements:

* **COMP-001**: The system should allow for batch entry of received items.
* **COMP-002**: The system must require a mandatory Project ID and a signature/digital confirmation upon issuance.

Missing non-functional requirements:

* **NFR-001**: The system should provide real-time notifications to the Storekeeper when stock levels fall below the safety threshold.
* **NFR-002**: The system should have a built-in reporting module for generating comprehensive reports on material usage, stock valuation, and total cost of materials consumed.

Uncovered user scenarios:

* **US-001**: A Site Engineer wants to request a material transfer between two specific Project IDs/Site Locations.
* **US-002**: A Purchasing Officer wants to generate a Purchase Requisition (PR) based on low stock alerts or project needs.

Missing edge cases and error handling:

* **COMP-003**: The system should handle partial issue (e.g., 100 units requested, 95 units issued).
* **NFR-003**: The system should have a built-in audit trail for tracking all inventory transactions, including the user who performed the action and the timestamp.

Score: 8/10

**2. CONSISTENCY ANALYSIS**

Internal conflicts among requirements:

* **COMP-001** and **US-002**: Both require batch entry of received items, but US-002 also mentions generating a Purchase Requisition (PR) based on low stock alerts or project needs.
* **NFR-001** and **NFR-003**: Both mention real-time notifications, but NFR-003 requires an audit trail for tracking all inventory transactions.

Terminology inconsistencies:

* **COMP-001** uses the term "batch entry," while **US-002** mentions generating a Purchase Requisition (PR).

Conflicting assumptions or constraints:

* **NFR-001** assumes real-time notifications, but **NFR-003** requires an audit trail for tracking all inventory transactions.

Misaligned entities and relationships:

* **COMP-001** and **US-002**: Both involve Site Engineers, but COMP-001 focuses on batch entry of received items, while US-002 involves generating a Purchase Requisition (PR).

Score: 6/10

**3. CLARITY ANALYSIS**

Ambiguous requirements:

* **COMP-001**: The term "batch entry" is not clearly defined.
* **US-002**: The phrase "generate a Purchase Requisition (PR) based on low stock alerts or project needs" is unclear.

Unclear acceptance criteria:

* **NFR-001**: What constitutes a "real-time notification"?

Vague or imprecise language:

* **COMP-002**: The term "signature/digital confirmation" is not clearly defined.
* **US-002**: The phrase "generate a Purchase Requisition (PR) based on low stock alerts or project needs" is vague.

Missing definitions:

* **NFR-001**: What is the definition of "real-time notification"?

Score: 7/10

**4. CORRECTNESS ANALYSIS**

Requirements that do not match user stories:

* **COMP-001**: The system should allow for batch entry of received items, but this requirement does not align with any user story.

Technically unfeasible specifications:

* **NFR-001**: Real-time notifications may be technically challenging to implement.

Incorrect business rules:

* **US-002**: Generating a Purchase Requisition (PR) based on low stock alerts or project needs is an incorrect assumption, as it does not align with the user story.

Missing or wrong dependencies:

* **COMP-001**: The system should allow for batch entry of received items, but this requirement depends on other unspecified factors.

Score: 5/10

**5. TESTABILITY ANALYSIS**

Requirements that cannot be tested:

* **NFR-001**: Real-time notifications may be difficult to test due to their dynamic nature.
* **NFR-003**: The audit trail for tracking all inventory transactions is a complex requirement that may require specialized testing.

Missing measurable criteria:

* **COMP-001**: Batch entry of received items does not have clear, measurable criteria.

Unclear success conditions:

* **US-002**: Generating a Purchase Requisition (PR) based on low stock alerts or project needs has unclear success conditions.

Verification challenges:

* **NFR-001**: Real-time notifications may require specialized testing tools.
* **NFR-003**: The audit trail for tracking all inventory transactions requires complex verification procedures.

Score: 6/10

**6. TRACEABILITY ANALYSIS**

Requirements not traceable to user stories:

* **COMP-001**: Batch entry of received items does not align with any user story.
* **US-002**: Generating a Purchase Requisition (PR) based on low stock alerts or project needs is an incorrect assumption.

Missing requirement relationships:

* **NFR-001** and **NFR-003**: Both requirements involve real-time notifications, but NFR-003 requires an audit trail for tracking all inventory transactions.

Orphaned or duplicated requirements:

* **COMP-002**: The system must require a mandatory Project ID and a signature/digital confirmation upon issuance is a duplicate requirement.
* **US-001**: A Site Engineer wants to request a material transfer between two specific Project IDs/Site Locations is an orphaned requirement.

Gaps in impact analysis:

* **NFR-001** and **NFR-003**: Both requirements involve real-time notifications, but NFR-003 requires an audit trail for tracking all inventory transactions.

Score: 5/10

**7. OVERALL ASSESSMENT**

Overall Quality Score: 6.4/10
Ready for Implementation: No
Critical Issues Count: 3
Major Issues Count: 2
Minor Issues Count: 1
srs_document:
Here's a complete SRS document for an Inventory Management System:

# Software Requirements Specification
## Inventory Management System

### Document Information
- **Document Title:** Software Requirements Specification for Inventory Management System
- **Version:** 1.0
- **Date:** March 10, 2023
- **Domain:** Construction
- **Status:** Final

---

## Table of Contents
1. Introduction
2. Overall Description
3. System Features
4. External Interface Requirements
5. Non-Functional Requirements
6. System Models
7. Verification and Validation
8. Appendices

---

## 1. Introduction

### 1.1 Purpose
This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document outlines the requirements for an Inventory Management System designed to manage inventory levels, track stock movements, and provide real-time notifications.

### 1.2 Scope
The system will be developed using a combination of web technologies (HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript), backend frameworks (Node.js, Express.js), and databases (MongoDB). The scope includes the development of the following features:

* User authentication and authorization
* Inventory management (add, update, delete items)
* Stock tracking (track stock movements, low stock alerts)
* Reporting (generate comprehensive reports on inventory usage)

### 1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, and Abbreviations

| Term | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| SRS | Software Requirements Specification |
| HTML5 | HyperText Markup Language version 5 |
| CSS3 | Cascading Style Sheets version 3 |
| JavaScript | Programming language for client-side scripting |
| Node.js | Runtime environment for executing JavaScript code on the server |
| Express.js | Framework for building web applications using Node.js |
| MongoDB | NoSQL database management system |

### 1.4 References

* [Inventory Management System Requirements](https://example.com/inventory-management-system-requirements)
* [Construction Industry Standards](https://example.com/construction-industry-standards)

### 1.5 Overview
This SRS document is organized into the following sections:

* Overall Description (Section 2): Provides an overview of the system, its functions, and operating environment.
* System Features (Section 3): Describes the functional requirements of the system, including user authentication, inventory management, stock tracking, and reporting.
* External Interface Requirements (Section 4): Outlines the interface requirements for users, hardware, software, and communication interfaces.
* Non-Functional Requirements (Section 5): Specifies the performance, security, reliability, usability, maintainability, and portability requirements of the system.
* System Models (Section 6): Describes the data model, process model, and system architecture of the system.
* Verification and Validation (Section 7): Outlines the methods for verifying and validating the system's requirements.

## 2. Overall Description

### 2.1 Product Perspective
The Inventory Management System is designed to manage inventory levels, track stock movements, and provide real-time notifications for construction companies.

### 2.2 Product Functions
The system will perform the following functions:

* User authentication and authorization
* Inventory management (add, update, delete items)
* Stock tracking (track stock movements, low stock alerts)
* Reporting (generate comprehensive reports on inventory usage)

### 2.3 User Classes and Characteristics

| User Class | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Administrator | Responsible for managing user accounts, inventory levels, and reporting |
| Inventory Manager | Responsible for adding, updating, and deleting items in the inventory |
| Warehouse Staff | Responsible for tracking stock movements and low stock alerts |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
The system will be deployed on a cloud-based infrastructure with the following specifications:

* Hardware: Cloud-based servers (AWS, Google Cloud)
* Software: Node.js, Express.js, MongoDB
* Database: MongoDB

### 2.5 Design and Implementation Constraints
The system must comply with the following constraints:

* Security: Implement robust security measures to protect user data and inventory levels.
* Performance: Ensure the system can handle high traffic and large datasets.
* Scalability: Design the system to scale horizontally and vertically.

### 2.6 Assumptions and Dependencies

| Assumption | Description |
| --- | --- |
| User authentication | Users will be authenticated using a secure login mechanism. |
| Inventory data | Inventory levels will be updated in real-time. |

## 3. System Features

### 3.1 Functional Requirements
The following functional requirements are extracted from the user stories:

* **FR-001**: The system should allow users to log in and authenticate their identity.
	+ Priority: High
	+ Source: US-001
	+ Acceptance Criteria:
		- Users can log in using a secure login mechanism.
		- Users can access their account information after logging in.
* **FR-002**: The system should allow users to add, update, and delete items in the inventory.
	+ Priority: Medium
	+ Source: US-002
	+ Acceptance Criteria:
		- Users can add new items to the inventory.
		- Users can update existing items in the inventory.
		- Users can delete items from the inventory.

### 3.2 Business Rules

| Business Rule | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Inventory levels must be updated in real-time | The system will automatically update inventory levels when a user adds, updates, or deletes an item. |

## 4. External Interface Requirements

### 4.1 User Interfaces
The system will have the following user interfaces:

* Web interface: Users can access the system using a web browser.
* Mobile app: Users can access the system using a mobile app.

### 4.2 Hardware Interfaces
The system will interact with the following hardware devices:

* Barcode scanners: Used to scan barcodes and update inventory levels.
* Printers: Used to print reports and labels.

### 4.3 Software Interfaces
The system will integrate with the following software applications:

* Accounting software: Used to track financial transactions and inventory levels.
* CRM software: Used to manage customer relationships and sales data.

### 4.4 Communications Interfaces
The system will communicate with the following external systems:

* APIs: Used to retrieve and update inventory levels in real-time.
* Web services: Used to integrate with other applications and services.

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

### 5.1 Performance Requirements
The system must meet the following performance requirements:

* Response time: The system should respond within 2 seconds for all user interactions.
* Throughput: The system should handle up to 100 concurrent users.

### 5.2 Security Requirements
The system must meet the following security requirements:

* Authentication: Users must be authenticated using a secure login mechanism.
* Authorization: Users must have access control based on their roles and permissions.

### 5.3 Reliability Requirements
The system must meet the following reliability requirements:

* Availability: The system should be available 99.9% of the time.
* Fault tolerance: The system should be able to recover from failures and errors.

### 5.4 Usability Requirements
The system must meet the following usability requirements:

* User experience: The system should provide a user-friendly interface for users to interact with.
* Accessibility: The system should be accessible on various devices and browsers.

### 5.5 Maintainability Requirements
The system must meet the following maintainability requirements:

* Code quality: The code should follow best practices and be easy to understand.
* Documentation: The system should have comprehensive documentation for maintenance and support.

### 5.6 Portability Requirements
The system must meet the following portability requirements:

* Platform independence: The system should run on various platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux).
* Database independence: The system should use a database that is independent of the platform.

## 6. System Models

### 6.1 Data Model
The data model for the system includes the following entities and relationships:

| Entity | Description |
| --- | --- |
| User | Represents a user with attributes (username, password, role). |
| Item | Represents an item in the inventory with attributes (name, description, quantity). |
| Inventory | Represents the inventory levels of items with attributes (item ID, quantity). |

### 6.2 Process Model
The process model for the system includes the following processes and workflows:

* User authentication: Users log in to access their account information.
* Item management: Users add, update, or delete items in the inventory.
* Reporting: The system generates reports on inventory usage.

### 6.3 System Architecture
The system architecture includes the following components:

* Frontend: Web interface and mobile app for user interaction.
* Backend: Node.js and Express.js for server-side logic and API integration.
* Database: MongoDB for storing data.

## 7. Verification and Validation

### 7.1 Verification Methods
The following verification methods will be used to ensure the system meets its requirements:

* Unit testing: Test individual components of the system.
* Integration testing: Test how components interact with each other.
* System testing: Test the entire system for functionality and performance.

### 7.2 Validation Criteria
The following validation criteria will be used to ensure the system meets user needs:

* User acceptance testing (UAT): Users test the system for usability and functionality.
* Business acceptance testing (BAT): Business stakeholders test the system for business requirements.

### 7.3 Testing Requirements
The following testing requirements must be met:

* Test cases: Develop comprehensive test cases to cover all scenarios.
* Test data: Use realistic test data to simulate real-world scenarios.
* Test environment: Set up a test environment that mirrors production.

## 8. Appendices

### Appendix A: Traceability Matrix
| Requirement ID | User Story | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FR-001 | US-001 | The system should allow users to log in and authenticate their
